--- 
title: "Guide to Wise Investing"
author: "Junghoon Shin"
date: "`r paste(Sys.time(), 'KST')`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
csl: apa-with-abstract.csl
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
url: 'https\://junghoon-shin.github.io/'
github-repo: "junghoon-shin/guide-for-wise-investing"
cover-image: "images/cover1.jpg"
apple-touch-icon: "images/up-arrow.svg"
apple-touch-icon-size: 120
favicon: "images/up-arrow.svg"
description: "Investment data book containing up-to-date company financials, industry and market status, and global economy"
---

```{r, include = F}
# automatically create a bib database for R packages

knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(), "bookdown", "knitr", "rmarkdown"), "packages.bib")
```

```{r, include = F}
# initialize environment

library(pacman)

p_load(bookdown,
       ComplexHeatmap,
       cowplot,
       DT,
       furrr,
       ggpubr,
       googledrive,
       googlesheets4,
       grid,
       gridExtra,
       httr,
       kableExtra,
       knitr,
       magrittr,
       measurements,
       pander,
       RColorBrewer,
       scales,
       tidyverse,
       XML)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, out.width = "100%")

theme_update(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# define functions

read_html = function(url, which = integer(), header = T, trim = T) {
  readHTMLTable(rawToChar(GET(url)$content), which = which, header = header, trim = trim)
}

my_datatable = function(tbl, paging = F, pageLength = 5, scrollY = unit(700, "lines"), caption = NULL) {
  DT::datatable(tbl,
                options = list(dom = "lfrtip",
                               paging = paging,
                               pageLength = pageLength,
                               searching = T,
                               ordering = T,
                               scrollX = T, 
                               scrollY = scrollY,
                               fixedColumns = list(leftColumns = 1),
                               colReorder = list(realtime = F),
                               keys = T,
                               autoWidth = T,
                               initComplete = JS("function(settings, json) {", 
                                                 "$(this.api().table().header()).css({'font-size': '14px'});",
                                                 "}")),
                extensions = c("FixedColumns", "ColReorder", "KeyTable"),
              rownames = F,
              caption = caption) %>%
    formatStyle(columns = 1:ncol(tbl), fontSize = "14px")
}
```

# Preface {#preface .unnumbered}

![](images/cover1.jpg)

When I took my first step into the investment world, there was just too much information all over the newspapers, company websites, YouTube, books, and even lunch table gossips which all seemed to be of the utmost importance. I was so confused that I soon gave up to decide where to invest my money in on my own, and instead just tried to copy what some random 1M-subscriber stock YouTubers were doing. That strategy initially seemed fairly good, but then I read what [one of the most successful investors on the planet](#buffett) said: *"Risk comes from not knowing what you're doing."* It was painful because that was exactly what I was doing --- I didn't know what I was doing.

From that moment on I tried to read what the great investors wrote, collect information, distinguish between useful and useless ones as best as I could, and make my own decision where to invest whether it was correct or not. From those struggles this site was born. It initially aimed to serve as my personal resource of automatically updated financial data to guide unbiased and premeditated investing decision. As the project went along, it gradually progressed into a more public reference for the author's close friends who were interested in investing and seeking a financial data resource which was not [exhausting](https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/320193/000032019320000096/aapl-20200926.htm) but tidy and easy-to-get.

I have to mention that this project has been hugely inspired by [Warren Buffett](https://www.berkshirehathaway.com/letters/letters.html), @town2018, @graham2003, and @lee2020. This is a non-profit, public-access project still in its infant stage, and I am always open to suggestions from whoever hopes to make it better. Please feel free to [contact me](#author), or even better make a [pull request](https://docs.github.com/en/github/collaborating-with-issues-and-pull-requests/about-pull-requests) anytime anywhere. All data presented on this site are updated at least once a day. They were last updated at `r paste(Sys.time(), 'KST')`. Check them out daily and find out how easily investment can be incorporated into your life.\
\

![](images/CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0.png){width=120px}

This project is licensed under the [Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/).
